2606.12086.title: IntElicit: Eliciting and Assessing Contextualized Creativity via Dialogue Policy Optimization
2606.12086.paper_summary: Contextualized assessment offers high ecological validity for evaluating creativity but introduces a critical challenge: observed performance may be confounded with cognitive proficiency (domain knowledge) and agency (willingness to engage). Meanwhile, in the age of generative AI, creative problem solving increasingly occurs in tool-mediated and human--AI interactive environments, making fully static assessment less aligned with contemporary creative practice. To address these issues, this paper proposes IntElicit, a framework for eliciting and assessing contextualized creativity via dialogue policy optimization. IntElicit functions as a constrained adaptive AI Interviewer: it provides non-directive knowledge and agency scaffolds in multi-turn interaction to reduce non-creative confounders, while preserving participants' responsibility for generating the creative content being evaluated. Specifically, to tackle sparse rewards and potential reward hacking (e.g., answer dictation) in open-ended educational dialogue, IntElicit introduces a decomposed process reward mechanism. This mechanism aligns the policy with pedagogical elicitation, rewarding prompts that draw out participant reasoning rather than producing optimal answers on their behalf. Extensive experiments, including participant simulation and a human subject study (N=64), show that IntElicit improves elicited creative outcomes over expert-designed baselines. Together, the results suggest that interactive elicitation can reveal creative potential that static FPSP-style assessment may miss, providing a formative and diagnostic lens for contextualized creativity assessment in AI-mediated learning contexts.
2606.12086.paper_summary_zh: 情境評估為評估創造力提供了高生態效度，但也帶來了一個關鍵挑戰：觀察到的表現可能與認知能力（領域知識）和主動性（參與意願）混淆。<lb/>同時，在生成式人工智慧的時代，創造性問題解決越來越多地發生在工具介導和人類—AI互動的環境中，使得完全靜態的評估與當代創造性實踐的對應性降低。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，本文提出了IntElicit，一個通過對話政策優化來引出和評估情境創造力的框架。<lb/>IntElicit作為一個受限的自適應AI面試官運作：它在多輪互動中提供非指導性的知識和主動性支架，以減少非創造性的混淆因素，同時保留參與者對生成被評估創造內容的責任。<lb/>具體而言，為了應對稀疏的獎勵和潛在的獎勵駭客（例如，答案口述）在開放式教育對話中的問題，IntElicit引入了一個分解的過程獎勵機制。<lb/>這個機制使政策與教學引導相一致，獎勵引出參與者推理的提示，而不是代表他們產生最佳答案。<lb/>廣泛的實驗，包括參與者模擬和一項人類受試者研究（N=64），顯示IntElicit在引出創造性成果方面優於專家設計的基準。<lb/>總體而言，結果表明互動引導可以揭示靜態FPSP風格評估可能忽略的創造潛力，為AI介導學習情境中的情境創造力評估提供了一個形成性和診斷性的視角。
2606.12086.author: Mingjia Li et.al.
2606.12086.authors: Mingjia Li, Jin Wu, Hong Qian, Wenhao Huang, Yiyang Huang, Yiwen Zhang, Chanjin Zheng, Xiangfeng Wang, Aimin Zhou, Jiajun Guo
